name: Repeated TPE shifted human proteomic and immune aging markers
summary: A small human TPE study reported younger-direction movement in proteomic and immune-marker panels.
statement: Repeated therapeutic plasma exchange in a small human cohort was associated with younger-direction changes in proteomic, immune, and biological-age marker panels.
population_or_model: Adults undergoing repeated therapeutic plasma exchange
time_horizon: Repeated exchange course with post-treatment biomarker assessment
quantitative_note: The study supports human biomarker movement but is not treated as functional or disease-outcome evidence.
caveats: Small sample size and limited control structure constrain causal interpretation.
Biological-age and proteomic panels are surrogate endpoints.
Long-term durability and clinical meaning remain unresolved.
